title: Brooks Men's Ghost 14 Neutral Running Shoe
description: Smooth roads are ahead when you put on the Ghost 14. This award-winning runner favorite now features 100% DNA LOFT in the midsole, for a softer, smoother ride. We’ve also improved the fit so the shoe adjusts even more comfortably to your foot.
features: THIS MEN'S SHOE IS FOR: Runners looking for a smooth ride that won’t distract from the fun of the run. The Ghost 14 offers a simplified midsole construction which creates more seamless transitions. This Brooks Ghost 14 is a certified PDAC A5500 Diabetic shoe and has been granted the APMA Seal of Acceptance. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe.

SUPPORT AND CUSHION: The Ghost 14 offers neutral support while providing high energizing cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Ghost 13

BALANCED, SOFT CUSHIONING: BioMoGo DNA and DNA LOFT cushioning work together to provide a just-right softness underfoot without losing responsiveness and durability - yet it feels lighter than ever. DNA LOFT now makes up the entire midsole, for a softer, smoother, ride.
SMOOTH, STABLE RIDE: No matter how your foot lands, our Segmented Crash Pad - an integrated system of shock absorbers - will cushion every step and stride for smooth heel-to-toe transitions.
SOFT, SECURE, FIT: More strategic use of the 3D Fit Print upper technology provides structure that moves with the foot.

reviews.title: Brooks has lost a step on wear/quality, but still a very good shoe
reviews.text:
First, Brooks is a top running shoe manufacturer, and I always like buying their products, and they are to be applauded for releasing shoes in widths, which lets you get a shoe that fits, which is essential. If a shoe does not fit properly, then it will be extremely difficult to run (or walk any distance in). The Brooks Glycerin is one model up from the Ghost, that has better cushioning, but is no longer available in a Narrow width, and is more expensive, but the Ghost is a pretty good shoe on its own. I would have given the Ghost 5 stars, but the Ghost 14 is not quite perfect, having two areas where it could be better (and was better previously).

The first picture shows a Ghost 14 (on the left), next to a Ghost 13 (the previous model, on the right), and you can see the Ghost 14 has less tread, so wears out sooner, I would say roughly 50-75 miles of road (asphalt) running. The picture shows both shoes new. The Ghost 13 also was a little more cushioned, but you can offset that some by putting a different insole in the 14, and a lot of runners replace the insoles anyway. The thinner tread means less mileage on the 14s, but still probably as good as most of the competition, just no longer better here.

The second pictures shows the upper collar on a Ghost 14, and the fabric Brooks is using there (not so much on the 13 and earlier models) is nice and soft, but does not hold up. I generally see holes or failures at the top of the lace area, and on some shoes, towards the back collar area. You get a lot of fraying, sometimes holes, which is more an appearance problem than anything that would impact running in them, but means also you cannot wear them casually, at least where someone might see the failures. That may be an aspect that is supply chain related, they used to hold up better, but I have seen the problem on two pair of Ghost 14s, and other manufacturers do a bit better (probably needs a bit more nylon, to hold up longer).

I will say that the Ghost 14 has a narrower tongue at the top, that is shared with the 13 but was new there, and I have not seen it much in other shoes, but actually works very well, so I give kudos to Brooks on that design innovation. There is less chance that during a run the tongue will shift over into the forefoot, at least in a painful way, and you don't have to stop to shift it back.

So overall, particularly at this price point, the Brooks continues to be good value and one of the top shoes to consider, but as with anything, there are a couple of areas that could use further improvement.

title: VOCOOL 100 PCS 1/4inch Stainless Steel Track and Cross Country Spikes with Spike Wrench, Pyramid Replacement Track Spikes for Sprint Sports Short Running Shoes, Black/Silver
features: 【What You Get】: Package includes 100 pieces black spikes with 1pcs spike wrench, convenient for you to replace your shoe spikes. There are enough quantity for your daily use.

【Quality Material】: These spikes are made of quality hard steel, durable and rust-resist and anti-corrode, ensure you to use for a long time.Easy to match with most of the track shoes, improve your running balance of traction, flexibility and speed.

【Easy to Use & Replace Spikes】: The package includes a spike wrench. It is very easy for you to screw the track spikes into the shoes, convenient to replace the spikes. Ideal for athletic tracks, field and cross country surfaces.

【Size】: 6.35 mm (1/4 inch) in length, tapered and needle-shaped, which increases friction between the sole and the ground and prevents skidding,especially suit for track sprints, long jump,running, outdoor sports activities.

【Improve Running】: If you like running, climbing, or you are a sports enthusiast, this track spikes will help you a lot. This pyramid spikes will add the friction between the shoe sole and ground, thus can resist skid and improve your running speed.

reviews.title: WAY too tight over the foot.  NOT for Diabetics, IMHO!
reviews.text:
I've had these on for about an hour now, so I am sure they are not returnable. That's too bad, because an hour has shown me that they are very uncomfortable. I cannot imagine who would find them wearable. Maybe someone with abnormally narrow feet.

The VERY stretchy material they are made from makes them hard to get on. You MUST use a shoehorn, and even then, it is not easy. They also - unpleasantly - squeeze the foot. After an hour, my toes are getting cold from low circulation! Seriously! Plus, the material does not breathe well, despite it's "perforated" appearance. So my feet are hot and wet.

The laces are just decoration. It's the tight material that holds them on. Plus, the artsy-craftsy arch inset in the instep is an invitation to soiling underneath it, without giving much arch support.
Could these be knock-offs? I assumed the attractive price was because they are a discontinued color. Maybe they are just fakes!.

PS THESE ARE NOT FOR DIABETICS. There is too much restriction of circulation.

I was advised to get "caged" Ultraboosts...the ones that lace normally. (The "cage" is the plastic framework for the laces.) But I liked the color of these.

Big mistake.

Introducing the softest, most stable ride ever to come out of Mizuno running. Our new stability technology called smooth ride support is combined with cloud Wave to create a soft, lively and cushioned feel with adequate support and smooth guidance.
Since our founding in 1906, we at Mizuno have taken special pride and pleasure in being able to participate in the exciting world of sports and in making the entire globe the stage for our activities. Each and every one of us is guided by the ideal of true sportsmanship. That was true nearly 100 years ago and is still true today. It is evident in our corporate philosophy which, simply stated, is "Contributing to society through the advancement of sporting goods and the promotion of sports." This philosophy is clearly reflected in our strong support of numerous worldwide sport events and in our dedication to advancing sports through cutting edge technology and research. Our product development strategy is to create products with features that enhance athlete performance and are unique to only Mizuno. These technological advancements make our sporting equipment and apparel go the extra mile and keep you in tip top playing form no matter the sport nor the conditions. Whether it's our Breath Thermo line of clothing which generates heat from the absorption of moisture to keep you warm in cooler temperatures, to our line of Arctic Touch garments that expels heat and moisture generated by the body during exercise to keep your body cool and comfortable, your comfort and performance are at the forefront of our innovation. From the diamond to the track, from the court to the fairway, Mizuno's commitment to Serious Performance shines through. Today, the high quality equipment that only Mizuno can produce are relied upon by athletes throughout the world. We produce superior quality equipment for serious performance, through the artful combination of technology and craftsmanship. Because, at Mizuno, your passion is our obsession.

reviews.title: Comfortable, sturdy and a great value.
reviews.text:
First things First. I received this as a review item in exchange for an unbiased review. I have an 11 M foot (US) and while I wasn't overly crazy about the color, I realized they matched a lot of my race wear. So I decided to give them a try. The first run was a little tough but that's always the case (If you're new to running, trust me, don't run your first race without adequately breaking in your shoes). After about 15 miles I started to really like them.

So, you see the colors. I personally always buy the clip on laces which I did butI've probably got well over 60 miles on them now and while I'm pretty loyal to another brand (which is also costs substantially more) I will definitely be picking up another pair.

If you are a heel striker (work on your form before you suffer the consequences of it) these have ample cusioning in the back - but take a friendly word of advice, instead of getting into bad form. These are great shoes to improve your form with and once you have it, the extra padding in the back is not impediment. They are very light and fit well once worn in. I'm not trilled about the simultaneously yet cool look they have but so is life. You don't buy running shoes to look cool unless you're a poster you buy them for performance and these deliver.

Again I'm speaking to new runners and people that aren't that familiar with the sport. You don't want to have one pair you wear every day. They'll develop a wear pattern and it's not good. Instead buy at least two, ideally a few and alternate so that this happens at a much slower pace. From my experience, three pairs of shoes worn on alternating days will last much longer than one pair bought three times. And this is important b/c unless you're working on bearfoot running (which seems to still be a raging philosophical battle in the podiatric communities) you'll want support, comfort and stability. As a digression, I've tried barefoot running and it fundamentally changed the way I ran, until I stepped on a piece of glass and my right foot had yet to harden.

Again at the risk of lecturing remember that injuries are one of the biggest enemies to progress. You could easily run through broken glass on these and probably right over some smaller nails (I'm not advising it, just pointing it out). Styling aside I love these and will be picking up another pair or two in the near future.

description:

In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." I.G.S (Impact Guidance System) Technology: ASICS design philosophy that employs linked componentry to enhance the foot's natural gait from heel strike to toe-off.
Dynamic DuoMax Support System: This evolution of DuoMax system enhances stability and support, with reduced weight and increased platform support.
Rearfoot and Forefoot GEL Cushioning Systems: Attenuates shock during impact and toe-off phases, and allows movement in multiple planes as the foot transitions through the gait cycle.
FluidRide Midsole: FluidRide provides the ultimate combination of bounce back and cushioning properties with reduced weight and exceptional durability.
Guidance Trusstic System Technology: This Trusstic System integrates Guidance Line construction for enhanced gait efficiency while providing midfoot structural integrity.

reviews.title: Great shoe for an over-pronator like me
reviews.text: I've used the GT-2000 shoes over the last few years and have yet to have issue with them. I over-pronate pretty badly and this shoe offers great support for that issue; My knees thank me. I found the shoe to fit exactly true to size and for those reviews that say it's small, people tend to forget that your feet swell when you run, especially longer distances! My personal size foot is between a 6.5-7 and I typically wear a 7 shoe for running, dress, or sandals. I can't say I know the difference between the GT-2000 model numbers (1-5?) except for new-ness. I have no issue with wearing older models and like the cheaper prices. For reference, I run half marathons several times a year on city sidewalks, asphalt, dirt roads, and dirt trails.

reviews.title: Great value
reviews.text:
Purchased these gaiters for our hike through the Grand Canyon. Didn't install the velcro strap and it still worked wonders. My wife had the Outdoor Research version with a sole strap and was more frustrated due to that strap unlatching constantly....and this is half the price. I cant speak against its durability with abbrasions or water resistance. It feels as though it would be very helpful if caught in a rainstorm.

I should add I wore the L size over Salomon 4d Quest (size 11) with a lot of room to spare.

reviews.title: Secure but difficult to remove
reviews.text: Decent gaiters. Front lace hook is 'upside down'; you have to slide it in UNDER the lace. Good design for being secure, but let me tell you, it is a pain in the rear to take them off.

reviews.title: Lived up and exceeded expectations
reviews.text: We weren’t expecting as much snow on the trail today kept my upper sock area more warm and dry then without. I was really surprised. Super comfortable.

reviews.title: Gaiters a Hikers Best Friend
reviews.text: I purchased these gaiters for my Bug out Bag Gear. I am very happy with these gaiters! they seem to have good quality, as far as construction, and seem to be well made. It's a Hikers Find. I would Recommend these, and they are a little cheaper than Dirty Girl Gaiters. I am considering another purchase in the future, as I feel having more than one pair to change out the dirty ones in between laundering is a smart move. The patterns are limited and also color choices, but it's functionality is whats more important! I haven't put them on trail or hiking as of yet, but I intend to in the future!

reviews.title: Gaiters keep sand out of shoes
reviews.text: I super glued the velcro to the heel of my shoes as directed, secured my new gaiters and went on a multi-day hike, frequently in sand. The gaiters did a marvelous job of keeping the sand and debris out of my shoes. Also, they are packed really small to stash in my backpack when not in use. I would recommend these gaiters.

title: Brooks Men’s Launch 9 Neutral Running Shoe
features: Imported

THIS MEN’S SHOE IS FOR: The Launch 9 is for neutral runners who want a dependable, lightweight shoe with DNA cushioning and a highly breathable upper that helps runners go fast on every run. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe.
NEUTRAL SUPPORT: Lightweight, streamlined, with even more BioMoGo DNA cushioning, the Launch 9 offers neutral support to keep you running faster mile after mile.
ENHANCED BREATHABILITY: Reengineered upper features Creel Warp mesh, designed to be extremely light and breathable. Hints of reflectivity on the upper to increase visibility on the road.

LIGHTWEIGHT CUSHIONING: We have added more BioMoGo DNA cushioning to the midsole for an even faster ride without the extra weight. A dynamically responsive ride for days when you are working on your speed.
RESPONSIVE TRANSITIONS: Redesigned midsole increased by 2 mm to provide runners with more responsiveness while the Green Rubber Outsole helps your foot move quickly from heel to toe.

reviews.title: My new favorite running shoe.
reviews.text: Perfect fit right out of the box.  Wonderful cushioned run and very light weight.  Well made and no signs of wear even after several long runs.  Wide enough toe box to prevent any rubbing.  True to size.

reviews.title: BROOKS ARE The BEST SHOES HANDS DOWN

reviews.title: Get what you pay for
reviews.text:
I sent these back after trying on. They look longer than they are when on your feet, I assume it’s due to the design. Didn’t have the best feel and overall a great name but bad shoe.
I’d go with a better brooks or On Clouds

reviews.title: Very light and comfortable
reviews.text: Nice and light and comfortable which was what I wanted. I love how well they fit. Good price, too, for the quality.

reviews.title: Love 'em

reviews.title: Ok

reviews.title: Comfortable
reviews.text: Exactly what I expected. Good fit and very comfortable.

reviews.title: Great looking shoe.  Although, NOT for serious runners.
reviews.text: These shoes look fantastic and wear very well. They are NOT for running though, only for walking. Not enough cushion for serious runners.

reviews.title: Really Nice Fit! And a great deal!
reviews.text: I think one thing I like about my feet is that they are wide. And I can always find wide shoes for cheap. That's the case here. I got mine for a very reasonable price on sale. I've always loved New Balance for making wider shoes in the first place, but  these extra wide walking shoes are great. That's why I always go back to NB. They are not made as solid as before as these fabrics do tend to tear, make a hole on the big toe side, usually. But these fit great (size fit as expected), and breathes well.

title: Saucony Women's Kinvara 13 Running Shoe
description: When it comes to lightweight speed, nothing crushes the competition like the Kinvara. And this just so happens to be our lightest one yet. With more speed contouring and its signature flexible feel, it pushes you forward without holding anything back. These are the shoes you’ll do big things in.
features: Rubber sole
The Kinvara 13 was literally designed for speed. Its beveled midsole and new midfoot contouring deliver a smoother, faster ride.
Its re-imagined upper is made with a single-layer mesh, giving you a lightweight feeling and even more breathability.
We created the lightest Kinvara ever to help you truly take off. It’s 7.2 ounces of pure speed.

reviews.title: Light, neutral shoe
reviews.text: Saucony neutral running shoes are the only kind of running shoes that I have ever purchased. I had accepted that my feet can only tolerate running if I’m wearing the ugliest, clunkiest shoes. That’s changed with this pair. They are so lightweight and look nothing like my other pairs. I usually have to lace up my shoes differently, but haven’t had to do that with these. They’re so lightweight, but provide all of the support that I need

reviews.title: Get 1/2 size larger. I wear 8 1/2 purchased 9.
reviews.text: Comfortable and lightweight. Great color.

reviews.title: Lightweight and breathable
reviews.text:
Pros:
-Overall these shoes are pretty lightweight and breathable. Good amount of air flow with the mesh siding, however you need to take good care of these as the mesh can rip easier when you catch your foot on a step.
-I am a huge fan of saucony and have never had bad results with any of their shoes over the past 20 years. These shoes appear to be more of a basic model that I would expect to last about a year or 1 marathon season.
-No issues with blisters or rubbing.

Cons:
-There isn’t much grip/tread on the bottom of these shoes. For this reason, I don’t expect these to hold up as well as some of the other models of Saucony shoes I’ve had in the past.

reviews.title: Lightweight, cushioned, and Comfortable…
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:e8f84c743163d7b17341711cad808847]] These sneakers are true to size and very comfortable. Nice sneakers for light runs or jogging. Very breathable for summer too.

Trust you found this review honest and helpful:-)

reviews.title: True Fit
reviews.text: I have been wearing Saucony shoes for a few years now. They're one of the few shoe brands that I can order without trying on first. I can count on their shoes being true fit. This pair is no exception. I have wide feet and it's hard to find shoes that fit period! Let alone being able to order them online. These are running shoes and I am not a runner. I do find them to be less cushiony than I am used to. Maybe it's because they are running shoes. They are still comfortable to wear. I like the black and silver colors.

reviews.title: Comfortable
reviews.text: I got the package couldn’t believe how light weight it is. Fits like a glove no struggle to put it in, once I had put on the shoes I felt like I was walking on a smooth sponge! I can’t describe how comfortable this shoe is!! I highly recommend that everyone buy these!!

reviews.title: Too wide, too stretchy...
reviews.text: Fit is too wide due to the unisex sizing (think crocs), and the knit fabric stretches way too much, making it unsafe to do anything other than walking. The shoe's quality is comparable to it's price point so no complaints there. They are just not form fitting, so looks like a very loose floppy sock. Ended up going with a higher end knit sneaker. Would not buy or recommend.

reviews.title: They’re OK
reviews.text: You get what you pay for; as advertised but super cheap. The soles are made of foam, they wouldn’t hold up very well. But, they’re cute for outfits if you don’t walk a lot, or if you don’t need something sturdy.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: So far so good. No complaints. Very comfortable.

Compression mesh Energy Web contains & molds UA HOVR foam to give back the energy you put in. NEUTRAL: For runners who need a balance of flexibility & cushioning. Soft knit up.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: I really wanted to like these shoes. It's just completely uncomfortable.  There's no support. It feels cheap on. The side stripes are actually tighter than the rest of the shoe so it felt like my foot was being compressed. I have narrow feet and don't have the problem generally so it was very surprising to me. The heel is definitely higher thN the toe. I could feel that I was standing at an angle. I usually love adidas so I was pretty disappointed.

title: Salomon Women's Speedcross 3 Trail Running Shoe
description: Salomon was born in 1947 in the heart of the French Alps and the birthplace of modern alpinism. Driven by a passion for skiing and design innovation, Francois Salomon and his son George designed and perfected much of the first modern ski equipment. During the following 60 years, Salomon's commitment to innovative design and passion for mountain sports created a vast range of revolutionary new concepts in boots, skis and apparel and brought innovative solutions to footwear, apparel and equipment for hiking, adventure racing, mountaineering and trail running. Through performance-driven design, Salomon enhances mountain sports by converting new ideas into action and expanding the limits of possibility.
features: 100% Synthetic/Fabric
Rubber sole
Heel measures approximately 1.75"
Anti-debris mesh protects the foot from dirt, twigs, and pebbles
Contra grip is designed for better traction and durability on mixed trails and complicated landscapes
Water resistance adds an extra layer of protection from the elements
Lightweight material maximizes comfort, fit, and breathability
Collar shape and sole hardness is specifically designed for a woman's foot

reviews.title: Perfect trail shoe!
reviews.text: Bought these for my wife. Her words are: "I love them"! She says she has wide feet and they still fit perfectly, she wore them all day for a 14 mile mud run/obstacle course and had a great experience with these shoes. Great traction in the mud!

reviews.title: Great!
reviews.text: Love these shoes! So comfortable and well made!

reviews.title: Wife loves them.
reviews.text: I purchased these shoes for my wife as a gift for our trail hiking and she absolutely loves them! Wears them all day now.

reviews.title: Fit great and look good too
reviews.text: I own more than one pair of these, as the fit and support work well for me. They cradle the foot, especially under the arch, and fit more narrowly than many other sneakers (which makes them just right for me). I get many compliments on the appearance, which is streamlined and not at all "clunky."

reviews.title: Awesome shoe, my third pair in ten years.
reviews.text: The best.  Super secure on trail, uneven surfaces, great looking and very comfortable.  I won't wear anything else!

title: New Balance Women's W1260V5 Running Shoe
description: We stand for something bigger than sneakers. We champion those who are fearlessly driven by their passions. We elevate sport. We do right by people and the planet. Together, we drive meaningful change in communities around the world. We Got Now.

reviews.title: Runs a tad large, order 1/2 size down.. still 5+ stars!!!!
reviews.text: I have bought New Balance 1260’s for years and I am always a size 10.  This shoe was too big as a 10 and I had to return for a 9.5.  That being said, I still give it 5 stars and would give more if I could!  These were by far the most comfortable, light weight, and best looking (better than the pic by far) New Balance I’ve ever owned. I ordered in another color the next day!  Love these shoes.

reviews.title: Great shoes though
reviews.text: Even though I ordered wide width they are a little more snug across the ball of my foot than my other new Balance shoes of the same size.  Great shoes though.  Very comfortable and lightweight.  Love the color too!

reviews.title: These v5's are even more comfortable and have a sleeker narrower fit
reviews.text: Wow! I have 2 pair of the 1260v4 that are my absolute favs.  These v5's are even more comfortable and have a sleeker narrower fit. I love the color and the attention to details.

reviews.title: Very comfortable shoes!
reviews.text: I loved these shoes the first moment I put them on my feet.  They are so comfortable and provide so much support.  You really get your money's worth in these shoes.  They are like walking on clouds.  I walked in a 5K recently and they were amazing and my feet felt great afterwards. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Great style!
reviews.text: Great Shoe! I have received compliments on the style!
